title: androsace centriberica subsp. assoana
intro: Androsace centriberica subsp. assoana, also known as the Alpine rose, is a flowering plant belonging to the Primulaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and succulent leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Androsace centriberica subsp. assoana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
